_doc: E2 gold set — 30 items per docs/e2-rag-preregistration.md §4 (24 in-corpus: 8 fee-rule + 8 ucp-schema + 6 glossary + 2 cross-source; 6 out-of-corpus whose correct outcome is ABSTAIN). Fable-designed (plan §E2 route tag), committed AFTER the index commit and BEFORE any scoring; queried ONLY in the one scoring pass, EXPOSED afterward, never re-scorable. Leakage screens per AMENDMENT A3 are machine-checked by evals/rag/rag-gold-composition.test.ts. m1Rule: 'any' = at least one expectedFile in top-5 counts as an M1 hit; 'all' (cross-source) = every expectedFile must appear. M2 support = a cited span contains at least one expectedSpan verbatim.

items.question: How large can the charge for bringing an order to the customer be, as a share of what the order cost?
expectedSpans: delivery fee ≤ 15% × purchase price, per order

items.question: When can a platform bill more than three percent for payment handling?
expectedSpans: transaction fee > 3% allowed ONLY if = actual processor charge AND proof available to dept + establishment

items.question: What happens if a platform overcharges on a capped fee but returns the money a few weeks later?
expectedSpans: over-cap on a/b/d is not a violation if refunded within 30 days of the month-end

items.question: How many kinds of charges may appear on a restaurant's bill, and what happens to anything outside them?
expectedSpans: fee category ∈ {delivery, basic service, transaction, enhanced}; any other fee is unlawful

items.question: Under what condition may a platform charge for premium extras such as marketing placement?
expectedSpans: charging an enhanced fee ⇒ platform also offers (and charges a basic service fee for) the basic service

items.question: How much advance warning must a restaurant get before its rates change?
expectedSpans: a fee change takes effect ≥ 30 days after notice to the establishment

items.question: What monthly paperwork about charges does the law require platforms to give restaurants?
expectedSpans: monthly itemized list of each transaction incl. every fee charged, for the prior month

items.question: What protection against being buried in search results does a restaurant get when it pays the listing charge?
expectedSpans: paying a basic service fee ⇒ searchable on all platforms, not omitted from relevant results

items.question: What three pieces of information are mandatory for a product entry in the shopping formats?
expectedSpans:
"id",
    "title",
    "price"

items.question: Which named kinds of cost line does the totals structure recognize as standard?
expectedSpans: Cost category. Well-known values: subtotal, items_discount, discount, fulfillment, tax, fee, total.

items.question: Must a price reduction line carry a positive or negative amount in the cost breakdown?
expectedSpans: "amount": { "exclusiveMaximum": 0 }

items.question: What standard reasons can a business give when it cannot fulfill a requested product?
expectedSpans:
"not_found",
    "out_of_stock",
    "item_unavailable"

items.question: What identifier lets a browsing session's basket be converted into a purchase flow?
expectedSpans: Cart ID to convert to checkout. Business MUST use cart contents (line_items, context, buyer) and MUST ignore overlapping fields in checkout payload.

items.question: If the buyer's locale signals are missing, how does the merchant decide location-based pricing?
expectedSpans: Falls back to geo-IP if omitted.

items.question: In what units are product prices expressed?
expectedSpans: Unit price in ISO 4217 minor units.

items.question: Which top-level parts must a shopping basket object always include?
expectedSpans:
"ucp",
    "id",
    "line_items",
    "currency",
    "totals"

items.question: What is the difference between following the spec and telling the truth, in this project's terms?
expectedSpans: Conformance = the feed matches the SPEC (fields, types, schema). Truth = the feed matches REALITY (the SOR).

items.question: Before an AI referee's rulings are relied on, what has to be measured and published?
expectedSpans: Measuring an LLM judge's precision/recall/agreement against a labeled gold set before trusting its verdicts; the measurement is frozen and cited.

items.question: What does the principle about never checking your own work mean in this system?
expectedSpans: the party producing an artifact must not be the party verifying it

items.question: Where does the true value of a fact officially live, and what is that source called?
expectedSpans: The authoritative data source for a fact domain

items.question: What is it called when a published copy of prices or availability stops matching the source?
expectedSpans: Any divergence between a serving copy (feed, listing, agent-visible catalog response, fee statement line) and the SOR

items.question: What must a reported mismatch carry with it before it counts as a finding?
expectedSpans: A reported discrepancy that carries machine-checkable pointers to both sources

items.question: Which charge type has no monthly-average fallback and no refund grace period, and how does the glossary describe what it covers?
expectedSpans: | Transaction fee | **3%** | purchase price / order | **No** | **No** |
the card-processing-type fee; capped at 3% of the purchase price per order

items.question: The rulebook says one fee tier is only allowed alongside the plain tier — which two tiers are those and what are their caps?
expectedSpans: charging an enhanced fee ⇒ platform also offers (and charges a basic service fee for) the basic service
a fee for services beyond the basic service, chargeable only if the platform also offers the basic-service tier; capped at 20%

items.question: What dollar penalty does the city charge a platform that violates the caps?
How do California's delivery fee limits compare with New York's?
What commission rate does the largest delivery platform actually charge merchants today?
Which version of the checkout capability added support for recurring subscription billing?
What are the tax withholding requirements on platform payouts to restaurants?
How many businesses have been fined under the fee-cap law so far?
